petitioner_background: The petitioner is a self-represented individual seeking classification as an employment-based immigrant under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Act as an alien of extraordinary ability. The decision does not specify the petitioner's field of work, nationality, or career details. The petition was filed at the Vermont Service Center (EAC 05 072 50398).
outcome_reasoning: The appeal was summarily dismissed because the petitioner failed to specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact on appeal, as required by 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v). On appeal, the petitioner merely stated that her works had gained a highly recognized reputation and requested reconsideration, but did not submit a brief, evidence, or arguments addressing the pertinent regulatory criteria at 8 C.F.R. § 204.5(h)(3). Because the petitioner did not address the reasons stated for denial and provided no additional evidence, summary dismissal was required.
fraud_or_procedural_issues: Procedural: The appeal was summarily dismissed under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v) because the petitioner failed to specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact. The petitioner indicated she was not submitting a separate brief or evidence, and the appellate submission was unaccompanied by arguments or evidence addressing the regulatory criteria at 8 C.F.R. § 204.5(h)(3).
